title: On the Architecture of Character
author: From: The Portrait of a Lady by Henry James
content: There are people who make of their lives a work of art, and there are those who simply let life happen to them. Character is the ability to choose your own path amidst the noise.

title: Parkinson’s Law
author: From: C. Northcote Parkinson
content: Work expands to fill the time available for its completion. If you give yourself a week to finish a task, it will take a week.

title: The Focusing Illusion
author: From: Daniel Kahneman
content: Nothing in life is as important as you think it is while you are thinking about it. Heightened focus creates false significance.

title: The Flywheel Effect
author: From: Jim Collins
content: There is no single 'killer app' or 'magic moment' in success; it is the accumulation of quiet, disciplined effort.

title: Constraint-Based Creativity
author: From: The Design of Everyday Things
content: Unlimited choices cause paralysis; constraints force innovation. Designing within boundaries is the key to clarity.
